page: Amazon Web Services p6-b300.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel processors with 192 virtual central processing units derived from 96 physical cores. The dedicated processors operate at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz and support hyperthreading with two threads per core.

This instance is equipped with 4096.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory allocation of 21.33 GB per physical core. Local storage consists of 30400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, offering high-speed local read and write capabilities for large datasets. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 350 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer across distributed environments.

The primary acceleration capability of the p6-b300.48xlarge is delivered by eight NVIDIA B300 GPUs, which provide a combined total of 2149 GB of video RAM. This massive GPU memory pool and parallel processing capacity are designed to handle intensive computational workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the consolidation of massive system memory, extensive NVMe SSD storage, high-speed networking, and eight enterprise-grade GPUs into a single instance reduces the overhead of managing multi-node clusters for large-scale tasks. This hardware profile is engineered for workloads such as large language model training, complex deep learning, high-performance computing, large-scale data science, and massive database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services p6-b300.48xlarge is a high-performance, GPU-accelerated instance designed for intensive computational workloads. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel cores running at 2.4 GHz, paired with 4096.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA B300 GPUs providing a total of 2149 GB of VRAM. For high-speed data access, the instance includes 30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 350 Gbps. This dense resource allocation makes the instance highly efficient for large-scale parallel processing without the latency of multi-node clustering. It is optimized for demanding workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, high-performance computing, and massive database management.
og_description: Amazon Web Services p6-b300.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 192 Intel vCPUs, 4096 GB RAM, eight NVIDIA B300 GPUs with 2149 GB VRAM, and 30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services p6-b300.48xlarge features 192 Intel vCPUs, 4096 GB RAM, 8 NVIDIA B300 GPUs, and 30.4 TB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ultra-dense GPU instance featuring eight accelerators, massive system memory, and high-capacity local NVMe storage for complex parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA B300 GPUs with 2149 GB of total VRAM for parallel acceleration
192 Intel vCPUs and 4096.0 GB of system memory for high-capacity processing
30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Baseline network bandwidth of 350 Gbps to support distributed workloads
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
Optimized for large language model training, deep learning, and high-performance computing
